articles.title: "Feels like COVID lockdown": Intense heatwave leaves Khajuraho's UNESCO temples, roads deserted; businesses shut
articles.description: Khajuraho experiences a severe heatwave. Temperatures are soaring to nearly 47 degrees Celsius. Normal life and tourism have come to a standstill. Streets are deserted. Businesses are shut. The situation resembles a lockdown. Authorities are providing water and shaded areas. Heatwave conditions are expected to continue for another week across many parts of India.

articles.description: Somewhere between childhood and adulthood, an unannounced transfer occurs. A parent’s unspoken fear becomes our ambition, a family’s definition of what counts as a life well lived becomes our standard.

His availability bolsters New York's offensive and rebounding power.

The technology promises to reshape roles and create new opportunities across various sectors.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by renowned physicist Stephen Hawking: 'Life would be tragic if...'- The greatest physicist's most powerful quote was not about science, rather about finding humor in human struggle
articles.description: Quote of the Day: Stephen Hawking, a renowned physicist, is remembered not only for his groundbreaking scientific contributions but also for his profound observation: 'Life would be tragic if it weren't funny.' Despite facing a devastating illness for decades, Hawking's intelligence, curiosity, and humor allowed him to endure unimaginable physical limitations, offering a powerful lesson in resilience and perspective.

articles.title: MP police increase reward for information about Twisha Sharma's absconding husband to Rs 30,000

articles.title: LIVE: Asha Bhosle gets Guard of Honour; family & fans pay final respects at Shivaji Park, Mumbai
articles.description: Legendary singer Asha Bhosle was given a Guard of Honour as final rites were held in Mumbai. Visuals from the ceremony show emotional scenes as family members, including her granddaughter Zanai, were seen breaking down. The mortal remains were reportedly wrapped in the Tricolour as fans and well-wishers gathered to bid farewell.

articles.title: India-US trade surges elevenfold in two decades, crosses $220 billion mark: Sergio Gor
articles.description: At the Annual Leadership Summit of the American Chamber of Commerce, US Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor says, "... India’s expanding manufacturing ecosystem, digital infrastructure, innovation capabilities, and skilled talent pool complement America’s leadership in technology, investment, advanced research, and entrepreneurship. Our shared ambition of achieving $500 billion in bilateral trade by 2030 reflects the connectivity between our economies, the confidence built, and the scale of opportunity. We stand at a remarkable moment. Over the last two decades, bilateral trade has grown from about $20 billion to over $220 billion in goods and services — an elevenfold increase that reflects deep trust and strengthened economic integration. Today, the United States and India are among each other’s top trading partners... I am impressed by the individuals who visit the US Embassy each week. CEOs from the United States are here, choosing India over other parts of the world. Every week, top executives arrive — from Uber to Walmart, and recently Boeing, Lockheed, and GE. This is not a one off occurrence but a regular pattern, a testament to the limitless potential between our two countries."

articles.description: The filing showed SpaceX posted a loss of $4.9 billion in 2025 on revenue of $18.7 billion. Losses accelerated further in the first quarter of this year, with the company reporting a $4.3 billion loss on revenue of $4.7 billion.

articles.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party is stepping into uncharted territory by nominating B B Gopakumar, an MLA from Chathannoor, for the Speaker's chair in Kerala's assembly election, which takes place this Friday. This nomination represents a pivotal moment for the BJP in the state.

articles.description: Texas Tech University celebrated a unique graduate this week as service dog Sadie received an honorary diploma alongside her owner, Makaela Muse. The internet erupted with joy, praising Sadie's crucial support throughout Muse's college journey.

articles.title: How many push-ups should a 40-year-old man really be able to do?
articles.description: Hitting 40 and wondering about your fitness? A simple push-up test offers insights. While 16 reps is a baseline for men, experts emphasize personal progress over arbitrary numbers. Achieving more push-ups correlates with better cardiovascular health, but consistency and gradual improvement are key. Your starting point and ability to improve are the true measures of fitness.

articles.title: PM Modi Returns From Europe, Calls Crucial Ministerial Meeting Amid West Asia Crisis
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has convened a crucial meeting of the Council of Ministers at Seva Teerth in Delhi, just hours after returning from his Europe tour. All Union ministers have been asked to remain in the national capital ahead of the high-level discussions, making the meeting politically significant.Sources say the ongoing West Asia crisis and its possible economic impact on India are expected to dominate the agenda. The government is closely monitoring concerns linked to crude oil prices, fuel supply chains and inflation amid escalating tensions in the region. A high-powered informal group of ministers led by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh is already reviewing the evolving situation. Home Minister Amit Shah, Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman and Petroleum Minister Hardeep Singh Puri are part of the group overseeing preparedness measures. The meeting has also intensified speculation around a possible Cabinet reshuffle and expansion in the Modi 3.0 government ahead of its first anniversary on June 10. Sources indicate that the BJP leadership is reviewing ministerial performance and organisational functioning before any major exercise. The developments have made today’s meeting one of the most closely watched political and governance discussions in recent weeks

articles.description: The danger for the GCC economies, and indeed the global economy, is that fragmentation and chronic instability become structural rather than episodic

articles.title: Pritam reacts to Cocktail 2 song ‘Mashuqa’ plagiarism accusations, calls critics 'self-appointed music detectives'
articles.description: Pritam has reacted to the plagiarism accusations surrounding the Cocktail 2 song “Mashuqa,” strongly dismissing critics as “self-appointed music detectives.” The composer expressed frustration over repeated comparisons and claimed such allegations accompany every new release.

articles.title: ‘Adopt a Stray Instead’: Viral Husky Post Sparks Debate on Keeping Exotic Dogs in India’s 45°C Heat
articles.description: A viral social media post has ignited a debate in India about the ethics of owning exotic dog breeds like Siberian Huskies in hot climates. The discussion began after a user criticized a neighbor for keeping a Husky in a constantly air-conditioned room to survive 45°C heat.
